title: Basic connectivity recovery
body: Check whether the charger display is powered and the connector is fully seated.
If the charger appears online but the session will not start, stop and restart the charging session once.
If the problem persists, escalate to remote support instead of retrying repeatedly.

title: Local responder connectivity SOP
body: Confirm the charger screen is powered and no visible damage is present before touching site controls.
Run the approved local restart or isolation checklist once using site SOP.
If the charger does not recover immediately, stop local action and escalate to remote ops.

title: Remote connectivity checks
body: Verify charger heartbeat or last-seen status from the backend if available.
Confirm whether the charger was recently power-cycled or lost Wi-Fi connectivity.
If the charger is unreachable and no local resolver is available, queue technician escalation.

title: Safety-critical technician instructions
body: Treat the charger as potentially hazardous and isolate access to the unit.
Inspect for cable, connector, or enclosure damage before any restart attempt.
Document visible hazard evidence and prioritize on-site inspection.
